theory.canonical_statement: ID 450 defines the thermal preparation of the generator. To support superconducting state stabilization, the housing is transitioned to liquid helium temperatures. This cooldown must be gradual to avoid thermal shock to the 153,600 node lattice. Achieving this baseline reduces environmental noise, increasing coherence times beyond standard thermal predictions. Thermal Ballast: Noble gas buffering is introduced to facilitate dissipation of Poynting flux residue. Meissner Readiness: Reaching 4.2 K ensures superconducting rails are prepared for quantum flux pinning.
